original_image_tags: Two black-and-white diagrams illustrating the physics of light refraction in air layers, captioned "Drawing by Al Evers".

elements.description: A schematic diagram titled 'With a temperature inversion, light bends in refracting layer of air.' It depicts a cross-section of the atmosphere showing a light source on the right, rays of light bending downward, and labeled layers: 'WARM AIR', 'REFRACTING AIR LAYER', and 'COLD SURFACE'.

judge.reason: Description A claims there are 'two' diagrams with a caption 'Drawing by Al Evers', while Description B describes a single schematic diagram with a completely different title and no mention of the quoted caption, representing a fundamental disagreement on the number of figures and specific quoted text.
